prompt_queue.prompt: Bootstrap the repository's technical, developer-facing living documentation from source code. Treat this as a first-run documentation bootstrap. First build a coverage map from README.md, docs/**, source directories, tests, examples, issues, and pull requests. Complete bootstrap coverage includes every major public API, extension point, runtime contract, workflow/lifecycle path, data contract, storage/persistence boundary, auth/permission behavior, failure mode, operational workflow, and contributor-facing design principle, with any intentionally uncovered area explicitly marked out of scope. The default bootstrap output is a documentation pull request that creates or updates a docs index plus separate topic pages for major source areas and includes the coverage map in committed documentation. Create concise topic pages with clear titles, bounded scope, and cross-links from the index. Use create_or_update_github_file before create_github_pull_request. If tool, token, or writable-scope limits constrain the pass, state the partial coverage in the committed docs and pull request body. Use no_changes when existing docs already include a complete, navigable technical docs surface and an explicit coverage map across the full codebase.
